title: Richly Spiced Indian Lamb Kidney Curry
description: A citrusy and cheesy curry featuring lamb kidney, served in a banana leaf vessel, this dish is perfect for a special occasion and is Buddhist-temple-friendly as it does not contain any animal by-products except for the lamb kidney itself, which can be considered a by-product of the meat industry and thus not directly related to the killing of animals, the ferment adds a unique twist to the flavor profile.
instructions: Heat the ghee in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the cumin seeds and let them sizzle for a few seconds.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic and cook for another minute.
Add the curry powder and garam masala and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the lamb kidney pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the yogurt and lemon juice, and stir well to combine.
Reduce the heat to low and let the curry simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the lamb is cooked through.
Meanwhile, prepare the banana leaves by cutting them into large squares and washing them in cold water.
To serve, place a portion of the curry onto a banana leaf square, and fold the leaf over the curry to form a packet.
